scope.summary: Rule 15: a gate verdict is usable only when its recorded SHA exactly equals the candidate HEAD and its status is pass; absent, mismatched, and non-pass verdicts are explicit refusals, never usable verdicts.
out_of_scope: the gate commands themselves (cargo fmt/clippy/test) — only the verdict-reading/push-authorization logic changed

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh --selftest
entry_points.trace: All 19 self-tests pass, including the four verdict-state cases: 'an absent verdict is refused and names the candidate SHA', 'a verdict whose sha is HEAD is accepted', 'a matching verdict that is a failure is refused', 'a verdict describing another commit is refused and names both SHAs'.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh gate-verdict
entry_points.trace: Manual throwaway-repo check of all four states: absent -> exit 65 'no verdict recorded for <candidate>'; mismatched SHA -> exit 65 'no verdict recorded for <candidate>; a verdict describing <record-sha> exists'; matching fail -> exit 65 'is fail, not pass'; matching pass -> exit 0 prints the verdict. shellcheck clean.

limitations: verified-push's re-read guard is exercised by the failed-gate and recorded-verdict self-tests; the race between write_verdict and the re-read is defended in code but not induced under a live concurrent writer in CI
